label: Heavy going
summary:
A tense Sunday as the April Iran–Israel truce frayed to breaking point. After Israeli warplanes struck Beirut's southern suburbs — killing two and wounding around 20 in a Hezbollah stronghold, in what Israel called retaliation for Hezbollah drone fire on northern Israel — Iran launched waves of ballistic missiles toward Israel, the first direct Iran-to-Israel exchange since the spring ceasefire. Israel said it would hold fire 'for the moment' but warned it was ready to resume; Tehran said its attacks would restart if strikes on southern Lebanon continued. Lebanese officials pressed for talks over war, with parliament speaker Berri signaling Hezbollah would pull back from the Litani if Israel withdrew. Markets were shut for the weekend, but the exchange set up a sharp move in oil — Brent would jump above $97 on Monday — as the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz dragged on. Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces hit 26 Russian military, energy and logistics targets across occupied territory and Bryansk.

Against the gloom, the human-interest news leaned brighter. More than a million people flooded central Madrid for Pope Leo XIV's open-air Corpus Christi Mass, the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years. Armenians voted in a closely watched parliamentary election, handing PM Pashinyan's pro-Western Civil Contract a governing majority despite observers alleging Russian interference. Germany's Alexander Zverev won his first Grand Slam at the French Open in a five-set thriller over Italy's Flavio Cobolli. The week also brought a pancreatic-cancer pill that doubled survival in trials and data showing global forest loss outside the tropics fell 14%. The darker domestic note: a shooting near Toledo's Old West End Festival in Ohio wounded 12, with gunmen still at large.

drivers.headline: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs, killing two and wounding ~20 in a Hezbollah stronghold; Tehran warns of retaliation
Iran fires missiles toward Israel in first direct exchange since the April ceasefire; Israel says it is ready to resume strikes
Israel and Iran trade strikes, imperiling fragile ceasefire on the war's 100th day
Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces strike 26 Russian military, energy and logistics targets across occupied territory and Bryansk
Sudan's pound slides past 4,000 to the dollar as Ethiopian Airlines' dollar-only ticketing sparks fresh economic alarm
Weekend shooting near Toledo's Old West End Festival in Ohio wounds 12; gunmen still at large as Sunday festivities are cancelled
Oil set to surge as weekend Israel–Iran exchange and the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz keep Brent elevated
More than a million flood central Madrid for Pope Leo XIV's open-air Corpus Christi Mass — first papal visit to Spain in 15 years
Armenians vote in a high-stakes election; Pashinyan's pro-Western party wins a majority despite alleged Russian interference
Alexander Zverev beats Flavio Cobolli in five sets to win his first Grand Slam at the French Open
Pancreatic cancer pill daraxonrasib doubles median survival in trials as global forest loss outside the tropics falls 14%
Europe removes a record 603 river barriers in 2025, reconnecting roughly 2,300 miles of waterways for migrating fish
